title: Archived - Hard-surface disinfectants and hand sanitizers (COVID-19): Disinfectants and hand sanitizers accepted under COVID-19 interim measure - Canada.ca
content:
## Archived - Hard-surface disinfectants and hand sanitizers (COVID-19): Disinfectants and hand sanitizers accepted under COVID-19 interim measure
- List of disinfectants (COVID-19)
- List of hand sanitizers
- Information for manufacturers
- Products accepted under interim measure
On February 16, 2023, Health Canada announced that the interim measure for hand sanitizers expired. As a result, changes were made for importing and selling hand sanitizers accepted under the COVID-19 interim measure. For more information on these changes, please read the February 16, 2023, Drug Establishment Licensing Bulletin.
- Other interim measure requirements
- List of products accepted under interim measure
Health Canada implemented the interim measure process in March 2020 to meet the urgent need for hand sanitizers and hard-surface disinfectants.
We moved eligible surface disinfectants previously accepted under the interim measure process to the list of biocides for exceptional importation and sale . Our industry page provides further details on the requirements for importing and selling designated biocides.
As of January 15, 2021, we stopped accepting new notification forms for the importation of hand sanitizers through this interim measure.
Health Canada is implementing the following for hand sanitizers accepted under the COVID-19 interim measure:
- importation of hand sanitizers will be permitted until April 21, 2023
- distribution of these products to retail facilities will be permitted until August 18, 2023
- retail sale and use at the institutional level will be permitted until the stock has been exhausted or has expired

## Other interim measure requirements
As an interim measure due to the COVID-19 pandemic, we made it possible for certain products to be sold in Canada that may not fully meet regulatory requirements, but do not compromise the safety of Canadians. For example, these are products that:
- may have: English-only labelling, with conditions outlined below different packaging from what was authorized for sale in Canada
- English-only labelling, with conditions outlined below
- different packaging from what was authorized for sale in Canada
- are authorized or registered in other jurisdictions where the regulatory frameworks and quality assurances are similar
- keep records in the event of a product recall
- meet the following labelling requirements:
- packaging of imported product may be in French-only or English-only, or English or French and another language however, importers are required to post the bilingual label text on their website this website should not contain any additional advertising or claims outside what is authorized, for these products, in the antiseptic skin cleanser monograph
- however, importers are required to post the bilingual label text on their website
- this website should not contain any additional advertising or claims outside what is authorized, for these products, in the antiseptic skin cleanser monograph
- importers must provide sellers with a means to inform consumers, at time of sale, of where they can access bilingual information on the product
- this could be made available through a sticker applied directly to the products, posters or signage with take-away pamphlets at the point-of-sale
This interim measure does not apply to products using technical-grade ethanol in alcohol-based hand sanitizers.
Inclusion on this list does not constitute Health Canada's endorsement. This is not a comprehensive list of all hand sanitizers that may be appropriate for use. You can find more information on all other approved disinfectants, hand sanitizers and other drug or natural health products on our searchable Drug Product Database and Natural Health Products Database .

## List of products accepted under interim measure
The following is a list of products that are authorized for sale in Canada under the interim measure. Some of these products will not have a DIN or NPN on the label.
Please refer to the list of biocides for exceptional importation and sale for the list of designated biocides.
Any product associated with false or misleading claims or found to pose a risk to health is subject to enforcement action by Health Canada, including refusing shipments at the border and product seizures.
Please report disinfectants or hand sanitizers that don't have a DIN or NPN on the label and are not listed below or on the list of biocides for exceptional importation and sale . Use the complaint form to report to us. Also report any side effect or adverse event you experience from a health product.
As with all health products, always read and follow the directions on the product label. Never eat or drink hand sanitizers, as ingesting even small amounts can be dangerous or fatal.
